priorityLegend.P1: High-value, flat implementation difficulty; ideal first widening batch.
priorityLegend.P2: High/medium value, moderate implementation or mutation semantics.
priorityLegend.P3: Important parity but riskier, more stateful, larger schemas, or policy-bound.

requests.name: GetCanvasList
requests.description: Gets an array of canvases in OBS.

responseFields.valueDescription: Array of canvases

requests.name: GetPersistentData
requests.description: Gets the value of a "slot" from the selected persistent data realm.

requestFields.valueDescription: The data realm to select. `OBS_WEBSOCKET_DATA_REALM_GLOBAL` or `OBS_WEBSOCKET_DATA_REALM_PROFILE`
The name of the slot to retrieve data from

responseFields.valueDescription: Value associated with the slot. `null` if not set

requests.name: SetPersistentData
requests.description: Sets the value of a "slot" from the selected persistent data realm.

requestFields.valueDescription: The data realm to select. `OBS_WEBSOCKET_DATA_REALM_GLOBAL` or `OBS_WEBSOCKET_DATA_REALM_PROFILE`
The name of the slot to retrieve data from
The value to apply to the slot

requests.name: GetSceneCollectionList
requests.description: Gets an array of all scene collections

responseFields.valueDescription: The name of the current scene collection
Array of all available scene collections

requests.name: SetCurrentSceneCollection
requests.description:
Switches to a scene collection.

Note: This will block until the collection has finished changing.

requestFields.valueDescription: Name of the scene collection to switch to

requests.name: CreateSceneCollection
requests.description:
Creates a new scene collection, switching to it in the process.

Note: This will block until the collection has finished changing.

requestFields.valueDescription: Name for the new scene collection

requests.name: GetProfileList
requests.description: Gets an array of all profiles

responseFields.valueDescription: The name of the current profile
Array of all available profiles

requests.name: SetCurrentProfile
requests.description: Switches to a profile.

requestFields.valueDescription: Name of the profile to switch to

requests.name: CreateProfile
requests.description: Creates a new profile, switching to it in the process

requestFields.valueDescription: Name for the new profile

requests.name: RemoveProfile
requests.description: Removes a profile. If the current profile is chosen, it will change to a different profile first.

requestFields.valueDescription: Name of the profile to remove

requests.name: GetProfileParameter
requests.description: Gets a parameter from the current profile's configuration.

requestFields.valueDescription: Category of the parameter to get
Name of the parameter to get

responseFields.valueDescription: Value associated with the parameter. `null` if not set and no default
Default value associated with the parameter. `null` if no default

requests.name: SetProfileParameter
requests.description: Sets the value of a parameter in the current profile's configuration.

requestFields.valueDescription: Category of the parameter to set
Name of the parameter to set
Value of the parameter to set. Use `null` to delete

requests.name: GetVideoSettings
requests.description:
Gets the current video settings.

Note: To get the true FPS value, divide the FPS numerator by the FPS denominator. Example: `60000/1001`

responseFields.valueDescription: Numerator of the fractional FPS value
Denominator of the fractional FPS value
Width of the base (canvas) resolution in pixels
Height of the base (canvas) resolution in pixels
Width of the output resolution in pixels
Height of the output resolution in pixels

requests.name: SetVideoSettings
requests.description:
Sets the current video settings.

Note: Fields must be specified in pairs. For example, you cannot set only `baseWidth` without needing to specify `baseHeight`.

requestFields.valueDescription: Numerator of the fractional FPS value
Denominator of the fractional FPS value
Width of the base (canvas) resolution in pixels
Height of the base (canvas) resolution in pixels
Width of the output resolution in pixels
Height of the output resolution in pixels

requests.name: GetStreamServiceSettings
requests.description: Gets the current stream service settings (stream destination).

responseFields.valueDescription: Stream service type, like `rtmp_custom` or `rtmp_common`
Stream service settings

requests.name: SetStreamServiceSettings
requests.description:
Sets the current stream service settings (stream destination).

Note: Simple RTMP settings can be set with type `rtmp_custom` and the settings fields `server` and `key`.

requestFields.valueDescription: Type of stream service to apply. Example: `rtmp_common` or `rtmp_custom`
Settings to apply to the service

requests.name: GetRecordDirectory
requests.description: Gets the current directory that the record output is set to.

responseFields.valueDescription: Output directory

requests.name: SetRecordDirectory
requests.description: Sets the current directory that the record output writes files to.

requestFields.valueDescription: Output directory

requests.name: GetSourceFilterKindList

Similar to `GetInputKindList`

responseFields.valueDescription: Array of source filter kinds

UUID of the source

The kind of filter to be created
Settings object to initialize the filter with

Current name of the filter
New name for the filter

Name of the source
UUID of the source
Name of the filter

responseFields.valueDescription: Whether the filter is enabled
Index of the filter in the list, beginning at 0
The kind of filter

Name of the source the filter is on
UUID of the source the filter is on

Object of settings to apply

Name of the source the filter is on

requests.name: GetVersion
requests.description: Gets data about the current plugin and RPC version.

responseFields.valueDescription: Current OBS Studio version
Current obs-websocket version
Current latest obs-websocket RPC version
Array of available RPC requests for the currently negotiated RPC version
Image formats available in `GetSourceScreenshot` and `SaveSourceScreenshot` requests.
Name of the platform. Usually `windows`, `macos`, or `ubuntu` (linux flavor). Not guaranteed to be any of those
Description of the platform, like `Windows 10 (10.0)`

requests.name: GetStats
requests.description: Gets statistics about OBS, obs-websocket, and the current session.

responseFields.valueDescription: Current CPU usage in percent
Amount of memory in MB currently being used by OBS
Available disk space on the device being used for recording storage
Current FPS being rendered
Average time in milliseconds that OBS is taking to render a frame
Number of frames skipped by OBS in the render thread
Total number of frames outputted by the render thread
Number of frames skipped by OBS in the output thread
Total number of frames outputted by the output thread
Total number of messages received by obs-websocket from the client
Total number of messages sent by obs-websocket to the client

requests.name: BroadcastCustomEvent
requests.description: Broadcasts a `CustomEvent` to all WebSocket clients. Receivers are clients which are identified and subscribed.

requestFields.valueDescription: Data payload to emit to all receivers

requests.name: CallVendorRequest
requests.description:
Call a request registered to a vendor.

A vendor is a unique name registered by a third-party plugin or script, which allows for custom requests and events to be added to obs-websocket.
If a plugin or script implements vendor requests or events, documentation is expected to be provided with them.

requestFields.valueDescription: Name of the vendor to use
The request type to call
Object containing appropriate request data

responseFields.valueDescription: Echoed of `vendorName`
Echoed of `requestType`
Object containing appropriate response data. {} if request does not provide any response data

requests.name: GetHotkeyList
requests.description:
Gets an array of all hotkey names in OBS.

Note: Hotkey functionality in obs-websocket comes as-is, and we do not guarantee support if things are broken. In 9/10 usages of hotkey requests, there exists a better, more reliable method via other requests.

responseFields.valueDescription: Array of hotkey names

requests.name: TriggerHotkeyByName
requests.description:
Triggers a hotkey using its name. See `GetHotkeyList`.

Note: Hotkey functionality in obs-websocket comes as-is, and we do not guarantee support if things are broken. In 9/10 usages of hotkey requests, there exists a better, more reliable method via other requests.

requestFields.valueDescription: Name of the hotkey to trigger
Name of context of the hotkey to trigger

requests.name: TriggerHotkeyByKeySequence
requests.description:
Triggers a hotkey using a sequence of keys.

Note: Hotkey functionality in obs-websocket comes as-is, and we do not guarantee support if things are broken. In 9/10 usages of hotkey requests, there exists a better, more reliable method via other requests.

requestFields.valueDescription: The OBS key ID to use. See https://github.com/obsproject/obs-studio/blob/master/libobs/obs-hotkeys.h
Object containing key modifiers to apply
Press Shift
Press CTRL
Press ALT
Press CMD (Mac)

requests.name: Sleep
requests.description: Sleeps for a time duration or number of frames. Only available in request batches with types `SERIAL_REALTIME` or `SERIAL_FRAME`.

requestFields.valueDescription: Number of milliseconds to sleep for (if `SERIAL_REALTIME` mode)
Number of frames to sleep for (if `SERIAL_FRAME` mode)

requests.description:
Gets the audio monitor type of an input.

The available audio monitor types are:

- `OBS_MONITORING_TYPE_NONE`
- `OBS_MONITORING_TYPE_MONITOR_ONLY`
- `OBS_MONITORING_TYPE_MONITOR_AND_OUTPUT`

requestFields.valueDescription: Name of the input to get the audio monitor type of

requestFields.valueDescription: Name of the input
UUID of the input

responseFields.valueDescription: Object of audio tracks and associated enable states

requests.name: SetInputAudioTracks
requests.description: Sets the enable state of audio tracks of an input.

requestFields.valueDescription: Name of the input

Track settings to apply

requests.name: GetInputDeinterlaceMode
requests.description:
Gets the deinterlace mode of an input.

Deinterlace Modes:

- `OBS_DEINTERLACE_MODE_DISABLE`
- `OBS_DEINTERLACE_MODE_DISCARD`
- `OBS_DEINTERLACE_MODE_RETRO`
- `OBS_DEINTERLACE_MODE_BLEND`
- `OBS_DEINTERLACE_MODE_BLEND_2X`
- `OBS_DEINTERLACE_MODE_LINEAR`
- `OBS_DEINTERLACE_MODE_LINEAR_2X`
- `OBS_DEINTERLACE_MODE_YADIF`
- `OBS_DEINTERLACE_MODE_YADIF_2X`

Note: Deinterlacing functionality is restricted to async inputs only.

requestFields.valueDescription: Name of the input
UUID of the input

responseFields.valueDescription: Deinterlace mode of the input

requests.name: SetInputDeinterlaceMode
requests.description:
Sets the deinterlace mode of an input.

Note: Deinterlacing functionality is restricted to async inputs only.

requestFields.valueDescription: Name of the input
UUID of the input
Deinterlace mode for the input

requests.name: GetInputDeinterlaceFieldOrder
requests.description:
Gets the deinterlace field order of an input.

Deinterlace Field Orders:

- `OBS_DEINTERLACE_FIELD_ORDER_TOP`
- `OBS_DEINTERLACE_FIELD_ORDER_BOTTOM`

Note: Deinterlacing functionality is restricted to async inputs only.

requestFields.valueDescription: Name of the input
UUID of the input

responseFields.valueDescription: Deinterlace field order of the input

requests.name: SetInputDeinterlaceFieldOrder
requests.description:
Sets the deinterlace field order of an input.

Note: Deinterlacing functionality is restricted to async inputs only.

requestFields.valueDescription: Name of the input
UUID of the input
Deinterlace field order for the input

requests.name: GetInputPropertiesListPropertyItems
requests.description:
Gets the items of a list property from an input's properties.

Note: Use this in cases where an input provides a dynamic, selectable list of items. For example, display capture, where it provides a list of available displays.

requestFields.valueDescription: Name of the input
UUID of the input
Name of the list property to get the items of

responseFields.valueDescription: Array of items in the list property

requests.name: PressInputPropertiesButton
requests.description:
Presses a button in the properties of an input.

Some known `propertyName` values are:

- `refreshnocache` - Browser source reload button

Note: Use this in cases where there is a button in the properties of an input that cannot be accessed in any other way. For example, browser sources, where there is a refresh button.

requestFields.valueDescription: Name of the input
UUID of the input
Name of the button property to press

requests.name: GetMediaInputStatus
requests.description:
Gets the status of a media input.

Media States:

- `OBS_MEDIA_STATE_NONE`
- `OBS_MEDIA_STATE_PLAYING`
- `OBS_MEDIA_STATE_OPENING`
- `OBS_MEDIA_STATE_BUFFERING`
- `OBS_MEDIA_STATE_PAUSED`
- `OBS_MEDIA_STATE_STOPPED`
- `OBS_MEDIA_STATE_ENDED`
- `OBS_MEDIA_STATE_ERROR`

requestFields.valueDescription: Name of the media input
UUID of the media input

responseFields.valueDescription: State of the media input
Total duration of the playing media in milliseconds. `null` if not playing
Position of the cursor in milliseconds. `null` if not playing

requests.description:
Sets the cursor position of a media input.

This request does not perform bounds checking of the cursor position.

requestFields.valueDescription: Name of the media input
UUID of the media input
New cursor position to set

requests.name: OffsetMediaInputCursor

Offsets the current cursor position of a media input by the specified value.

This request does not perform bounds checking of the cursor position.

requestFields.valueDescription: Name of the media input
UUID of the media input
Value to offset the current cursor position by

- `OBS_BLEND_SCREEN`
- `OBS_BLEND_MULTIPLY`
- `OBS_BLEND_LIGHTEN`

Scenes and Groups

Scenes and Groups

requests.name: GetSceneList
requests.description: Gets an array of scenes in OBS.

requestFields.valueDescription: UUID of the canvas the scenes are in

responseFields.valueDescription: Current program scene name. Can be `null` if non-main canvas or internal state desync
Current program scene UUID. Can be `null` if non-main canvas or internal state desync
Current preview scene name. `null` if not in studio mode or non-main canvas
Current preview scene UUID. `null` if not in studio mode or non-main canvas
Array of scenes

requests.name: GetGroupList
requests.description:
Gets an array of all groups in OBS.

Groups in OBS are actually scenes, but renamed and modified. In obs-websocket, we treat them as scenes where we can.

responseFields.valueDescription: Array of group names

requests.name: GetCurrentProgramScene
requests.description:
Gets the current program scene.

Note 1: This request is slated to have the `currentProgram`-prefixed fields removed from in an upcoming RPC version.

Note 2: Canvases do not have any concept of a program or preview scene, so this request does not support canvases.

responseFields.valueDescription: Current program scene name
Current program scene UUID
Current program scene name (Deprecated)
Current program scene UUID (Deprecated)

requests.name: SetCurrentProgramScene
requests.description: Sets the current program scene.

requestFields.valueDescription: Scene name to set as the current program scene
Scene UUID to set as the current program scene

requests.name: GetCurrentPreviewScene
requests.description:
Gets the current preview scene.

Only available when studio mode is enabled.

Note: This request is slated to have the `currentPreview`-prefixed fields removed from in an upcoming RPC version.

responseFields.valueDescription: Current preview scene name
Current preview scene UUID
Current preview scene name
Current preview scene UUID

requests.name: SetCurrentPreviewScene
requests.description:
Sets the current preview scene.

Only available when studio mode is enabled.

requestFields.valueDescription: Scene name to set as the current preview scene
Scene UUID to set as the current preview scene

requests.name: CreateScene
requests.description: Creates a new scene in OBS.

requestFields.valueDescription: UUID of the canvas to create the new scene in. Leave default to assume main canvas

responseFields.valueDescription: UUID of the created scene

Name of the scene to be renamed
UUID of the scene to be renamed

requestFields.valueDescription: UUID of the canvas the scene is in, if using the sceneName field

requests.name: SetSceneSceneTransitionOverride

requests.name: GetSourceActive
requests.description:
Gets the active and show state of a source.

**Compatible with inputs and scenes.**

requestFields.valueDescription: UUID of the canvas the source is in, if using sourceName field
Name of the source to get the active state of
UUID of the source to get the active state of

responseFields.valueDescription: Whether the source is showing in Program
Whether the source is showing in the UI (Preview, Projector, Properties)

requests.name: GetSourceScreenshot
requests.description:
Gets a Base64-encoded screenshot of a source.

The `imageWidth` and `imageHeight` parameters are treated as "scale to inner", meaning the smallest ratio will be used and the aspect ratio of the original resolution is kept.
If `imageWidth` and `imageHeight` are not specified, the compressed image will use the full resolution of the source.

**Compatible with inputs and scenes.**

requestFields.valueDescription: UUID of the canvas the source is in, if using sourceName field
Name of the source to take a screenshot of
UUID of the source to take a screenshot of
Image compression format to use. Use `GetVersion` to get compatible image formats
Width to scale the screenshot to
Height to scale the screenshot to
Compression quality to use. 0 for high compression, 100 for uncompressed. -1 to use "default" (whatever that means, idk)

responseFields.valueDescription: Base64-encoded screenshot

requests.name: SaveSourceScreenshot
requests.description:
Saves a screenshot of a source to the filesystem.

The `imageWidth` and `imageHeight` parameters are treated as "scale to inner", meaning the smallest ratio will be used and the aspect ratio of the original resolution is kept.
If `imageWidth` and `imageHeight` are not specified, the compressed image will use the full resolution of the source.

**Compatible with inputs and scenes.**

requestFields.valueDescription: UUID of the canvas the source is in, if using sourceName field
Name of the source to take a screenshot of
UUID of the source to take a screenshot of
Image compression format to use. Use `GetVersion` to get compatible image formats
Path to save the screenshot file to. Eg. `C:\Users\user\Desktop\screenshot.png`
Width to scale the screenshot to
Height to scale the screenshot to
Compression quality to use. 0 for high compression, 100 for uncompressed. -1 to use "default" (whatever that means, idk)

Kind of the transition
Whether the transition uses a fixed (unconfigurable) duration

Small note: While the namespace of scene transitions is generally unique, that uniqueness is not a guarantee as it is with other resources like inputs.

requests.name: OpenVideoMixProjector
requests.description:
Opens a projector for a specific output video mix.

Mix types:

- `OBS_WEBSOCKET_VIDEO_MIX_TYPE_PREVIEW`
- `OBS_WEBSOCKET_VIDEO_MIX_TYPE_PROGRAM`
- `OBS_WEBSOCKET_VIDEO_MIX_TYPE_MULTIVIEW`

Note: This request serves to provide feature parity with 4.x. It is very likely to be changed/deprecated in a future release.

Opens a projector for a source.

Note: This request serves to provide feature parity with 4.x. It is very likely to be changed/deprecated in a future release.

UUID of the source to open a projector for

Size/Position data for a windowed projector, in Qt Base64 encoded format. Mutually exclusive with `monitorIndex`

events.name: CanvasCreated
events.description: A new canvas has been created.

events.name: CanvasRemoved
events.description: A canvas has been removed.

events.name: CanvasNameChanged
events.description: The name of a canvas has changed.

events.name: CurrentSceneCollectionChanging
events.description:
The current scene collection has begun changing.

Note: We recommend using this event to trigger a pause of all polling requests, as performing any requests during a
scene collection change is considered undefined behavior and can cause crashes!

dataFields.valueDescription: Name of the current scene collection

events.name: CurrentSceneCollectionChanged
events.description:
The current scene collection has changed.

Note: If polling has been paused during `CurrentSceneCollectionChanging`, this is the que to restart polling.

dataFields.valueDescription: Name of the new scene collection

events.name: SceneCollectionListChanged
events.description: The scene collection list has changed.

events.name: CurrentProfileChanging
events.description: The current profile has begun changing.

events.name: CurrentProfileChanged
events.description: The current profile has changed.

events.name: ProfileListChanged
events.description: The profile list has changed.

events.name: SourceFilterListReindexed
events.description: A source's filter list has been reindexed.

events.description: A filter has been added to a source.

dataFields.valueDescription: Name of the source the filter was added to
The settings configured to the filter when it was created
The default settings for the filter

dataFields.valueDescription: Name of the source the filter was on

events.name: SourceFilterNameChanged
events.description: The name of a source filter has changed.

dataFields.valueDescription: The source the filter is on

events.name: ExitStarted
events.description: OBS has begun the shutdown process.

events.name: InputCreated
events.description: An input has been created.

dataFields.valueDescription: The unversioned kind of input (aka no `_v2` stuff)
Bitflag value for the caps that an input supports. See obs_source_info.output_flags in the libobs docs
The settings configured to the input when it was created
The default settings for the input

events.name: InputRemoved
events.description: An input has been removed.

events.name: InputNameChanged
events.description: The name of an input has changed.

events.name: InputSettingsChanged
events.description:
An input's settings have changed (been updated).

Note: On some inputs, changing values in the properties dialog will cause an immediate update. Pressing the "Cancel" button will revert the settings, resulting in another event being fired.

dataFields.valueDescription: New settings object of the input

events.name: InputActiveStateChanged
events.description:
An input's active state has changed.

When an input is active, it means it's being shown by the program feed.

events.name: InputShowStateChanged
events.description:
An input's show state has changed.

When an input is showing, it means it's being shown by the preview or a dialog.

events.name: InputMuteStateChanged
events.description: An input's mute state has changed.

events.description: An input's volume level has changed.

dataFields.valueDescription: New audio balance value of the input

events.name: InputAudioSyncOffsetChanged
events.description: The sync offset of an input has changed.

events.name: InputAudioTracksChanged
events.description: The audio tracks of an input have changed.

dataFields.valueDescription: Object of audio tracks along with their associated enable states

events.name: InputAudioMonitorTypeChanged

The monitor type of an input has changed.

Available types are:

- `OBS_MONITORING_TYPE_NONE`
- `OBS_MONITORING_TYPE_MONITOR_ONLY`
- `OBS_MONITORING_TYPE_MONITOR_AND_OUTPUT`

dataFields.valueDescription: New monitor type of the input

The UUID of the scene the item is in

New transform/crop info of the scene item

events.name: SceneCreated
events.description: A new scene has been created.

dataFields.valueDescription: Whether the new scene is a group

events.name: SceneRemoved
events.description: A scene has been removed.

dataFields.valueDescription: Whether the scene was a group

events.name: SceneNameChanged
events.description: The name of a scene has changed.

events.name: CurrentProgramSceneChanged
events.description: The current program scene has changed.

dataFields.valueDescription: Name of the scene that was switched to
UUID of the scene that was switched to

events.name: CurrentPreviewSceneChanged
events.description: The current preview scene has changed.

dataFields.valueDescription: Name of the scene that was switched to

events.name: SceneListChanged
events.description:
The list of scenes has changed.

TODO: Make OBS fire this event when scenes are reordered.

events.name: CurrentSceneTransitionChanged
events.description: The current scene transition has changed.

events.name: CurrentSceneTransitionDurationChanged
events.description: The current scene transition duration has changed.

events.name: SceneTransitionStarted
events.description: A scene transition has started.

events.name: SceneTransitionEnded
events.description:
A scene transition has completed fully.

Note: Does not appear to trigger when the transition is interrupted by the user.

events.name: SceneTransitionVideoEnded
events.description:
A scene transition's video has completed fully.

Useful for stinger transitions to tell when the video *actually* ends.
`SceneTransitionEnded` only signifies the cut point, not the completion of transition playback.

Note: Appears to be called by every transition, regardless of relevance.

events.name: StudioModeStateChanged
events.description: Studio mode has been enabled or disabled.

events.name: ScreenshotSaved
events.description:
A screenshot has been saved.

Note: Triggered for the screenshot feature available in `Settings -> Hotkeys -> Screenshot Output` ONLY.
Applications using `Get/SaveSourceScreenshot` should implement a `CustomEvent` if this kind of inter-client
communication is desired.

dataFields.valueDescription: Path of the saved image file

events.name: VendorEvent
events.description:
An event has been emitted from a vendor.

A vendor is a unique name registered by a third-party plugin or script, which allows for custom requests and events to be added to obs-websocket.
If a plugin or script implements vendor requests or events, documentation is expected to be provided with them.

dataFields.valueDescription: Name of the vendor emitting the event
Vendor-provided event data. {} if event does not provide any data

events.name: CustomEvent
events.description: Custom event emitted by `BroadcastCustomEvent`.

officialProtocol.sourceOfTruth: Official obs-websocket generated protocol JSON, not competitor implementations.

competitorSignals.caution: GPL, textified JSON, broad filesystem/raw/vendor exposure.

competitorSignals.signal: search/execute code-mode surface backed by 100+ OBS operations including canvases/events/animation.
competitorSignals.caution: Code execution is intentionally rejected for this repo; use only for parity discovery.
Storage, HTTP, automation, and screenshot retention are later product architecture work.

competitorSignals.signal: 7 action-multiplexed tools covering connection, recording, streaming, scene, source, audio, media.
competitorSignals.value: Window capture, VS Code packaging, screenshots, virtual cam.
competitorSignals.caution: Action multiplexing and static singleton connection conflict with this foundation.

competitorSignals.value: Good small public surface: status, scenes, record/stream, audio, profiles, screenshots.
competitorSignals.caution: Screenshot file writes and global profile/canvas changes need policy.

competitorSignals.signal: 18 high-level show/director tools plus state resource.
competitorSignals.value: Later workflow value: segments, overlays, highlights, safety modes, snapshots.
competitorSignals.caution: Depends on stable low-level core first.

competitorSignals.value: Presentation/demo automation ideas around text, media, filters, replay.

priorityImplications: Next low-risk/high-value verticals: record, stream, input audio/mute/volume, scene item list/enable.
Add discovery/status early: GetStats, input/source/scene-item lists, stream/record/output status.
Screenshots are high value but require payload/filesystem policy before default exposure.
Scene item transforms and source creation are valuable but need identity and broad-object schema work.
Config/profile/scene collection/canvas tools are global/stateful and should be deliberate.
Virtual camera and replay buffer are strong competitor signals after core stream/record.
Filters, transitions, media inputs, UI/projectors are parity-phase tools after low-risk status/control.
Events/resources/prompts/automation are product-layer work after request/response correctness.
Raw/vendor/batch APIs must be explicit opt-in lanes, never default LLM-facing tools.
gotchas: Gate by GetVersion.availableRequests, not version strings.
Keep explicit tool schemas; avoid action-multiplexed and code-execution surfaces.
Preserve stdout for MCP JSON-RPC; diagnostics to stderr only.
Screenshot save paths need allowlist or disabled-by-default policy.
Profile, scene collection, video settings, canvas, stream service, and record directory changes are global/stateful.
Multi-canvas v5.7 breaks simple name-only assumptions; include optional canvasUuid where protocol supports it.
OBS request failures must map cleanly to MCP errors with OBS status metadata.
Toolset filtering must unregister or block disabled handlers.
High-volume events need explicit opt-in, bounded buffering, throttling/coalescing, and separate toolset gating.
